name: Jimmy Rushing
description: American blues shouter and swing jazz singer (1901-1972)
abstract: James Andrew Rushing (August 26, 1901 – June 8, 1972) was an American singer and pianist from Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, U.S., best known as the featured vocalist of the Count Basie Orchestra from 1935 to 1948. Rushing was known as "Mr.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Count Basie, Jimmy Rushing, and Bobby Hackett

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Jimmy Rushing's band for a while. He had a good band, yes. He had myself, Emmett Berry, Dickie Wells, Buddy Tate.

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: You told her that in late ‘59 you were playing in Storyville with Buck Clayton and Jimmy Rushing? ... Yes. That’s right. That’s right. Jimmy was the singer, Buck, Vic Dickinson, Pee Wee Russell, Bud Freeman. That was the four front line.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Rushing. Yeah. I mean, Jimmy -- in fact from this thing, Jimmy was with Basie before Basie even -- Jimmy and Basie were working on a session or a gig or something before Basie even ever did lead a band.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote Same with Jimmy Rushing. /"Leave Me," and "How You Want Your Lovin' Done" on Vanguard. Jimmy and I fell out about those two tunes because...well, what happened was we were going to become a team

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: We had a manager named Maceo Birch, he travelled with the band, and Billie Holiday travelled with the band for a while when she first joined the band. Then, Jimmy Rushing he travelled with the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Now the Southern Serenaders... Jimmy Rushing was the singer... his people always owned a restaurant in Oklahoma City, and he made it possible for us to eat over there
